questions.question: VLOOKUP's third argument specifies:
questions.explanation: Col index num: 1=first col, 2=second col of the range.

questions.question: SUMIFS differs from SUMIF by:
questions.explanation: SUMIF: one condition. SUMIFS: multiple AND conditions.

questions.question: XLOOKUP improves over VLOOKUP by:
options: Searches both directions, returns any column, handles not-found
questions.explanation: VLOOKUP only looks rightward and breaks when columns are inserted.

questions.question: COUNTIFS counts rows where:
questions.explanation: All criteria must be simultaneously true.

questions.question: To freeze the first row while scrolling:
options: View -> Freeze Panes -> Freeze Top Row
questions.explanation: View tab -> Freeze Panes -> Freeze Top Row.

questions.question: INDEX-MATCH is preferred over VLOOKUP because:
options: It can return values to the LEFT and is not affected by column insertions
questions.explanation: VLOOKUP breaks if columns are inserted. INDEX-MATCH is more robust.

questions.question: IFERROR(formula, value) does what?
options: Returns value if the formula produces an error, otherwise the formula result

questions.question: A pivot table aggregates data by:
options: Summarising grouped by selected fields without formulas
questions.explanation: Drag fields to Rows/Columns/Values for instant cross-tabulation.

questions.question: INDIRECT() function is used to:
options: Convert a text string into a cell reference (dynamic references)
questions.explanation: INDIRECT enables dynamic range references built from strings.

questions.question: The $ symbol in $A$1 means:
options: Absolute reference -- row and column won't change when copied
